title: Forgetful Functor from Real to Complex AZ Classes
summary: Forgetting the antiunitary symmetries maps the real classes AI, D, AII, and C to complex class A and the real classes BDI, DIII, CI, and CII to complex class AIII; the resulting complex normalization explains why the s - delta = 4 real entry is labeled 2Z relative to the corresponding complex Z classification; source anchor: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects (lines 2024-2030).
assumptions: Scope: the early Section III relation between real and complex defect classifications.
This child records the class mapping and normalization role only, not the full later K-theoretic functorial derivation.
regime: Promoted from AITP topic chiu-classification-topological-quantum-matter-symmetries-1505-03535 via candidate candidate:forgetful-functor-real-to-complex-az-classes after passing the documented AI coverage and consensus gate.
scope: Can the forgetful-functor relation from real to complex AZ classes be promoted as a reusable equivalence map?
